programme.title: Most Haunted: Unseen: Samlesbury Hall P1
programme.desc: With a history dating back to 1185, Salmesbury Hall is of the UK’s most historic buildings and said to be one of the most haunted. With ghost stories and sightings all over the building, Stuart investigates the ground floor.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Unseen: Samlesbury Hall P2
programme.desc: Karl investigates the upper floors of this historic building dating back to 1185, and realises he is not alone.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Unseen: Gressenhall Workhouse Pt1
programme.desc: Karl investigates the very haunted school room in the large imposing Gressenhall Workhouse. The activity starts even before Karl gets into the building and becomes one of the most impressive investigations to date.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Unseen: Gressenhall Workhouse Pt2
programme.desc: Stuart investigates two buildings, a forge and the cottage. He could not have imagined the activity that unfolded around him.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Unseen: Bishton Hall P1
programme.desc: Stuart investigates the main house and seems to be pulled in different directions from an unexplained energy.

programme.title: Murdertown: Brighton
programme.desc: In 2015 a man visits a tarot card reader. Within an hour she’s dealt out the death card and he’s confessed to a murder. When cops arrive they discover he’s been living at home with his victim’s body for days.

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: Portsmouth: Ten-part true crime series with Katherine Kelly examining the UK's most shocking murders and the impact they've left on local communities. First up: Portsmouth.

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: Wrexham: Katherine Kelly explores how Jordan Davidson left an entire community in fear, when his series of increasingly violent crimes culminated in murder.

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: Middlesbrough: Katherine Kelly examines Middlesbrough murderer Reginald Wilson, described by criminal psychologists as one of the most dangerous men ever to be assessed.

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: Lorraine Thorpe hit headlines as Britain's youngest female double-killer. But more deaths were about to rock the Suffolk town. Katherine Kelly explores.

programme.title: Murdertown
programme.desc: Darlington: Katherine Kelly explores cannibal killer David Harker. One of the UK's most disturbed psychopaths, his crime rocked Darlington, causing a second horrific murder.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Malcolm Webster
programme.desc: The women in Malcolm Webster’s life seemed prone to bad luck. One died in a car crash. Another suffered a mysterious illness. A third, a keen sailor, found her life jacket punctured. How long before police realised this was more than coincidence?

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Stephen Searle
programme.desc: Stephen and Anne Searle were the grandparents at the helm of a large, close-knit family. But Stephen was having an affair, and Anne had been pushed to drink by her husband’s controlling behaviour. Neither was destined to enjoy a peaceful retirement.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Ann Marie Anastasi
programme.desc: Ann Marie and Anthony Anastasi had four children, a big house on a desirable street… and a younger lover living in their basement. It was a toxic love triangle, and one that was destined to end in tragedy.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Hannegret Donnelly
programme.desc: The Donnelly family’s neighbours knew they were unusual. Christopher and Hannegret were rarely seen outside the house, and the children were home-schooled. But no one could guess the violence taking place within their home - until it was too late.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Iain Lawrence
programme.desc: Ian and Sally Lawrence were a glamorous couple, and they looked like they had it all. But their marriage suffered when Ian’s star started to fall as Sally’s career rocketed. And when the couple suffered a terrible car crash, only one came out alive.

programme.title: Meet, Marry, Murder: Chaz Higgs
programme.desc: High-profile politician Kathy Augustine married nurse Chaz Higgs after a whirlwind romance. They were an unlikely match, and when their marriage hit the rocks, Chaz decided to use his medical training to commit an undetectable crime.

programme.title: Stalked: Someone's Watching: In the Spotlight
programme.desc: A well-respected TV news journalist in San Diego is stalked by a deranged fan who threatens rape and murder if she doesn't follow explicit instructions while on air.

programme.title: Stalked: Someone's Watching: Love is a Battlefield
programme.desc: A young nurse falls in love and marries the man of her dreams. But before long, her husband becomes violent and controlling.

programme.title: Stalked: Someone's Watching: Deadly Envy
programme.desc: An aspiring Hollywood actress is reacquainted with an old friend and fellow actress who becomes deeply jealous of her beauty and success.

programme.title: Stalked: Someone's Watching: Out of Bounds
programme.desc: A friendly nurse offers odd jobs to a struggling man who quickly becomes infatuated with her. Despite her rejections to his advances, the married man will not take "no" for an answer.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Bull's Eye
programme.desc: A Girl Guide leader is found dead on the floor of her bedroom. When an autopsy reveals a bullet lodged in her brain, detectives learn they’re investigating a murder.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Burning Obsession
programme.desc: In 1988, a promiscuous 22-year old boy from the Sikh community has one affair too many and ends up losing his life. Diligent detective work and DNA help police nail the killer who believed he had gotten away with murder.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Who Killed Santa?
programme.desc: In 2001, there are a series of murders of homeless people in Toronto. The latest killing is of a gentle man, well known in the area by residents who nicknamed him Santa Claus.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Angel of Death
programme.desc: An angel of mercy turns out to be an angel of death when the number of deaths in an Indiana Intensive Care Unit skyrockets. One astute nurse believes there must be more to this than just natural causes.

programme.title: 72 Hours: The Ghost
programme.desc: A cunning arsonist stays one step ahead of the police and terrifies a small farming community until behavioural profiling help detectives zero in on a very unlikely suspect.

programme.title: 72 Hours: Loch Ness Monster
programme.desc: In 1998, in a normally peaceful suburb, a pervert on a bicycle appears, performing sexual acts on himself and then attacking teenage girls. For reasons apparent to the residents, they nickname him after the Scottish Loch Ness monster.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers (daytime): Episode 7
programme.desc: Drag queen Devon feels snared by his barbed-wire tattoo because it's a painful reminder of an ex. Alice welcomes Halloween-loving Chippy, whose portrait of her daughter is more than a little spooky.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers (daytime): Episode 13
programme.desc: Harry wants a memorial tattoo dedicated to a very intimate part of himself after surviving testicular cancer. Matt has doodled on his own legs with a tattoo machine his parents got him for Christmas.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers (daytime): Episode 14
programme.desc: The artists cover Zoe's tattoo dedicated to her favourite food, Sean's lewd and lengthy leprechaun that his wife wants rid of, and Chantal's pitiful pop portrait that looks like a famous footballer.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers (daytime): Episode 15
programme.desc: Hayley's cheeky cartoon tattoo is threatening to derail her wedding plans. Mick wants to cover an X-rated inking that's causing embarrassment to his daughter. Dom's saucy camel is giving him the hump.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers (daytime): Episode 1
programme.desc: Jay saves Luke's bacon with a polished portrait. Sketch beautifies Mark's belly Buddha.

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 102
programme.desc: Myths tested: Is it possible to use a rowboat as a submarine? Can one escape if buried in the sand? What kind of projectiles would pirates use in a cannon if they run out of cannonballs?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 110
programme.desc: Does cabin fever exist? Does accelerating into a moose cause less damage than braking? Can a single stick of dynamite with a 20-second fuse be thrown, retrieved by a dog and detonate under an SUV?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 94
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can a baseball be hit hard enough to remove its covering? Does sliding into a base get a person there faster? Can balls be hit further with a corked bat?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 86
programme.desc: Every so often, the MythBusters draw conclusions that leave the fans seething. It’s time for the MythBusters to put more of their most contentious outcomes under the microscope.

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 26
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can too many bug bombs blow up a house? How hard is it to find a needle in a haystack? Will talking to plants help them grow?

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan receives orders to get a secret box off a plane that went down near Stalag 13.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hochstetter traces a defecting Field Marshall to Stalag 13.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan plants an empty parachute in the camp sending the Germans on a wild goose chase for a downed Allied agent.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan gets Klink to deal in supposedly rare cuckoo clocks so that he can send out information.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Newkirk poses as a traitor to send information via a propaganda radio broadcast.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan has to get Klink back into shape or Burkhalter plans to send him to the Russian Front.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Marya, the White Russian, uses Schultz to impersonate Goering just as Hogan is trying to save a trainload of stolen art.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Kinchloe participates in a camp boxing match to divert attention from Hogan's planned heist.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Burkhalter's niece is getting married and by having LeBeau design her wedding gown, Hogan is going to contact an underground agent, and later free him after capture by the Gestapo.

programme.title: The Steve Wilkos Show: I Punch a Woman like I Punch a Man
programme.desc: Chelse claims that her 19-month-old daughter is Malcolm's and that he beat her while pregnant. Amber and Phillip accuse each other of being abusive and want the polygraph tests to prove their claims!

programme.desc: Desmond believes that his ex Cassandra is making up a story about him touching their child because she's jealous of his new fiancée. And later, Tammy has been investigated for child abuse and even accused of locking her kids in a closet.

programme.title: The Steve Wilkos Show: Did You Ever Have My Baby?

programme.title: The Steve Wilkos Show: 16-Year-Old Surrogate Stole Our Baby
programme.desc: Sarah was Dawn and Derek's surrogate, but took off while five months pregnant. Sarah claims that they took advantage of her. Who are the baby's rightful parents?

programme.title: The Steve Wilkos Show: Teen Mom Murderer?

programme.title: The Steve Wilkos Show: Do I Have a Child With a Gay Man?
programme.desc: Are Tracy and Latonia's men leading secret double lives with other men? What will the lie detector test results reveal about Kanecha and Mike?

programme.title: The Adventures Of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: While on holiday, the detectives are impersonated for a profit.

programme.title: The Adventures Of Sherlock Holmes

programme.title: The Adventures Of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Holmes uncovers a serious mystery when helping one of Watson's old friends.

programme.title: The Adventures Of Sherlock Holmes

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes: The Case of the Unlucky Gambler

programme.title: The Spider Woman

programme.title: The Adventures Of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Holmes investigates a supposed suicide when the widow suspects it's mysterious.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay puts two teams of wannabe chefs through a series of gruelling challenges as they battle for the ultimate prize of a head chef position at his Las Vegas restaurant. The two teams are challenged to throw a party fit for a princess.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: The chefs compete to participate in a photo shoot and host a dinner for members of the Army.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: The two teams must create their own menus for the evening service. Despite a lacklustre selection, the female team threatens to outshine the men when it comes to food service.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: The chefs create ethnic dishes with select ingredients; a team falls behind before dinner service begins.

programme.title: Hell's Kitchen USA
programme.desc: Ramsay tests the chefs' palates with a blind taste test; Ramsay presents an unexpected challenge in order to determine who gets immunity.

programme.title: Half & Half: The Big Rules Of Engagement Episode

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Missouri Loves Company

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Half Pipe Dreams
programme.desc: 1970s Z-Flex Jimmy Plumer Poolrider skateboard with artwork by Doug Smith; Kevlar bulletproof vest, ballistic facemask and a helmet used by S.W.A.T. teams; a pair of 1990s racing pocket bikes capable of going 80 mph.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Brew-Phoria

programme.title: Auction Hunters: You Got Served
programme.desc: After a crushing defeat, the guys leave an auction empty handed and scramble to find new merchandise at a yard sale. But questionable antique tennis rackets and an ancient special effects box may not earn them a cent.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Hidden Hot Wheels
programme.desc: Ton and Allen challenge a big time auction buyer on his home turf. The guys uncover a 1969 Chevelle Malibu muscle car and a single-shot pistol designed to aid Allied resistance fighters during WWII.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Hula Moola
programme.desc: Ton and Allen jet to the Hawaiian islands where they're joined by a feisty new female assistant! The team uncovers a pair of James Bond-style gadgets designed for high-speed underwater travel and a vintage sled.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Night of the Digging Dead
programme.desc: A custom-built shark cage; 1900s pathologist's dissection toolkit; 1940s embalming pump and one of the first electric bone saws.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Rock and Roll

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Weapons of Past Destruction

programme.title: Teen Mom 2: Finale Special: Check Up With Dr. Drew
programme.desc: Dr Drew hosts the cast of Teen Mom 2 on-stage in front of a live studio audience. Jenelle, Chelsea, Kailyn and Leah reflect on the struggles of their first year of motherhood.

programme.title: Teen Mom 2: Season 1 Unseen Moment

programme.title: Teen Mom 2: Best Laid Plans
programme.desc: Jenelle is back with Kieffer after bailing him out of jail, but everything hits the fan when Barbara finds out that she's been lying about Kieffer and kicks her out.

programme.title: Teen Mom 2: Curveball
programme.desc: Leah's upset when Ali needs another MRI. Jenelle and Kieffer are living in her car but begin to fight. Chelsea decides to give Adam another shot.

programme.title: Teen Mom 2: Intensive care
programme.desc: Kailyn finds an apartment through a non-profit organization and moves out without telling her mum. After a huge fight where Kieffer pushed her, Jenelle decides to file domestic charges.

programme.title: The Maury Show: 7 Babies. 8 Men. Who's the Father?
programme.desc: These moms are desperate to find their children's missing fathers. But these denying dads say that there's no way they're the real fathers. When the DNA results are revealed, who will be proven right and who will be still searching for answers?

programme.title: The Maury Show: He's My Husband Now. Not Your Baby's Dad!
programme.desc: These guests say they're positive they know the true identity of the father of their children, but they just need a DNA test to prove it. Now the results are about to be revealed, but will they be the results they were hoping for?

programme.title: The Maury Show: I Cheated with 97 Women. Will My Fiancé Forgive Me?
programme.desc: Did Rey cheat on Ashley while she was pregnant with his baby and have a secret threesome behind her back? Plus, Jason is worried that his uncle is paying his fiancé to sleep with him. His uncle has a shocking confession that you have to see!

programme.title: The Maury Show: The Maury Show
programme.desc: It’s a Spring Wild Safari Spectacular on today’s MAURY! Jungle Jack Hanna joins Maury to share some of the most amazing animals we’ve ever seen! Your whole family will want to tune in to watch this amazing episode of MAURY!

programme.title: The Maury Show: We Had A Threesome…But I'm Not Your Baby's Dad!
programme.desc: These couples decided to have a threesome which ended up in a pregnancy. Now that the babies are born, these men deny they are the fathers. Today they will be forced to face the truth.

programme.title: The Maury Show: You Have 11 Kids…The DNA Will Prove It!
programme.desc: Teen mom Casie is dead set on proving Casey is the dad. Is Dirk the father of Brooke’s baby? Which brother fathered Miranda’s baby? Brandon denies both of Me'Chelle's kids but even his mother is on Me'Chelle's side!

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 6
programme.desc: Aang and Zuko are taken on parallel adventures that give them insight into their forefathers' pasts, but how does the tale of Roku and Sozin matter to them now?

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 7
programme.desc: When Toph discovers a quick way to make cash, Katara disapproves, and the rift between them has disastrous consequences.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 8
programme.desc: In a spooky village, Katara makes a special connection; the group investigate strange disappearances that occur during full moons.

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 9

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 10

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 11

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 12

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 14

programme.title: Avatar: The Last Airbender: Ep 15

programme.desc: Power of the Mind: A housewife has a strange and sudden vision that helps to solve a murder. A man remembers every single moment of his life.

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Amazing Survival

programme.title: Weird Or What? With William Shatner: Aliens Walk Among Us
programme.desc: Stories that suggest aliens are among us, including an Arizona woman who claims to have been fathered by an alien in a top-secret government experiment.

programme.title: UFOTV Presents: The Mysterious Stone Monuments of Markawasi Peru
programme.desc: Travel high into the Peruvian Andes to explore the strange, wondrous and unexplained mysteries of stone formations that may have been carved by thousands of years of erosion -- or the hands of a long-dead civilization

programme.title: UFOTV Presents: The Afterlife Investigations
programme.desc: Narrated by Britain's leading investigative Journalist - Donal MacIntyre - this award winning film presents four never before seen scientific investigations into life after death.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: PARANORMAL ACTIVITIES
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at paranormal activities including apparitions and ghosts, the dark day mystery, Marian Apparitions, curses, warnings from beyond, spontaneous combustion.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: ANCIENT MEGASTRUCTURES
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at ancient megastructures including the Olmec Heads of Central America, Moai of Eastern Island, Petra in modern day Jordan, the Alhambra in Spain, Angkor Wat in Cambodia, Chichen Itza.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: PLACES WITH HEALING POWERS
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at places that are said to have healing power including Lourdes where people have been cured by drinking water from a cave, Machu Picchu, Stonehenge, the Dead Sea, the Ganges.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MYSTERIOUS TEXTS
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at mysteries related to ancient texts including the Egyptian Book of the Dead, the Torah, the Secrets of Fulcanelli, the Tao Te Ching, the Vedas.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Bardo Museum, Tunisia
programme.desc: From the real lives of gladiators to the connection between Star Wars and Tunisia, discover the secrets inside the Bardo Museum.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the State Historical Museum, Moscow
programme.desc: From the duel that killed a genius to Lenin’s Rolls-Royce, discover the secrets inside the State Historical Museum.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Chateau of Versailles, Paris
programme.desc: From the schemes of Marie Antoinette to the spectrum of a star that reveals the origin of human life, discover the secrets inside the Chateau of Versailles.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Uffizi Gallery, Florence
programme.desc: From a famous artist who vanished to a traitorous Nazi who saved the day, discover the secrets inside the Uffizi Gallery.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the State Hermitage Museum
programme.desc: From ancient mummies who sport tattoos to re-examining who really killed Rasputin, discover the secrets inside the State Hermitage Museum.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Natural History Museum, London
programme.desc: From Darwin’s original specimens to a lost workshop that inspired James Bond, discover the secrets of Britain’s Natural History Museum.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Wild Water
programme.desc: In this episode, George investigates the wild side of water in dangerous class 5 river rapids, a flooded cave, and even in the ocean surrounded by massive Great White Sharks.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Midwest Stormfest
programme.desc: This season the tornadoes begin early - in February. Through the season, George follows the big weather from Missouri west to the Dakotas, and dodges the huge, destructive Greensburg tornado in southern Kansas.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Island Caving
programme.desc: Accompanied by a group of hardcore cavers from Alberta and British Columbia, George descends into spectacular caves that lead deep into the earth on Vancouver Island. The team carries inflatable kayaks to explore a dark underground river.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Hurricane
programme.desc: Linking up with legendary storm chaser Jim Leonard, George flies to Jamaica to track Hurricane Dean. George then travels to Nova Scotia to experience the even bigger turmoil of Hurricane Noel at the iconic Maritime village of Peggy's Cove.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Waterspouts
programme.desc: In the Florida Keys, George uses a motorboat, a van and small plane to get up close to waterspouts - the nautical cousins of tornadoes. He also meets some of the 25 million alligators that live in the Sunshine State.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Hawaii: Surf & Snow
programme.desc: In Hawaii George explores the wild side: diving with Galapagos sharks, ascending to snow-covered Mauna Kea, dental cleaning by Peppermint Cleaner Shrimp, extreme surfing at Banzai Pipeline, getting his boots lit on fire by Kilauea volcano.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Thunder Down Under
programme.desc: In Australia George teams up with storm chaser Jimmy Deguara to witness summer storms in the outback. While in Oz, he dives the magnificent Great Barrier Reef, and explores just how painful is the sting of the venomous Box Jellyfish.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Hottest and Coldest
programme.desc: George meets ultra-marathon runners in searing heat of Death Valley and bone-chilling cold of the Yukon. He dog-sleds in snow and meets wild characters like Caveman Bill, who has lived alone in a cave through 12 long cold Yukon winters.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Italia Wild
programme.desc: In Italy, George studies avalanches of Dolomite Mountains, meets engineers who keep Venice from sinking, and heads South to explore Italy's explosive volcanoes: Vesuvius, Etna and Stromboli.

programme.title: Naked Science: Making Mountains
programme.desc: The Rockies. Magnificent and awe-inspiring mysterious, the Rockies are one of the longest mountain ranges in the world. Naked Science investigates the geological processes that build these massive mountains.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Martian Attractions
programme.desc: The Latest in Martian exploration from orbit and on the ground, new sources of water and planetary dust storms are a few of the stories unfolding on to the Red Planet.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Shore To Shore
programme.desc: Our seafaring nature has translated well to the space age, seeking to sail out into the unknown in search of riches of one kind or another.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Interface
programme.desc: The Ionisphere, where the Earth interfaces with space, is little understood, it effects radio communications, GPS signals and satellites alike.

programme.title: The New Frontier: The Inner Worlds
programme.desc: They orbit between us and our Star, the inner worlds; hidden within the sun's glare often only visible at sunrise or sunset.

programme.title: The New Frontier: From There To Here
programme.desc: Series introduction and the study of Earth from orbit.

programme.title: The New Frontier: The Ice Giants
programme.desc: Beyond the asteroid belt lay the ice giants, some can be seen with the naked eye, others only glimpsed once by a passing probe.

programme.title: The New Frontier: PEERING INTO THE ABYSS
programme.desc: Imaging a black hole was a breakthrough in astronomy, now with the new satellite IXPE high energy telescope we will be looking at some of the most dramatic, and most violent objects in the universe: Colliding Black holes and Neutron Stars.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Return To The Moon
programme.desc: The Moon, our closest celestial companion. Our indispensible dance partner through the cosmos.

programme.title: The New Frontier: Slingshot
programme.desc: Sending probes throughout the solar system is a mathematicians dream and an engineers nightmare, sling shotting probes around other planets to arrive at their destination.

programme.title: RISING TIDES
programme.desc: The next generation of Earth observation satellites are now in place maintaining the continuity of long-term climate data, in particular the planets rising temperatures and related ocean levels.

programme.title: Wildest Indochina: Malaysia – Freaks of Nature
programme.desc: Malaysia is one of a handful of countries in the world described as ‘mega-diverse’. It’s a land of two halves - West Malaysia is on the mainland, the tip of Indochina’s peninsula, and Eastern Malaysia is the top portion of Borneo.

programme.title: Wildest India: India's Lost World
programme.desc: North-east India is an anthropological paradise; there is no other place on Earth with so many different ethnic groups. The forest are filled with mysterious tribes whose lives are dictated by the rain and the seasonal fruits of the forest.

programme.title: Meerkat Manor: Rise of the Dynasty
programme.desc: Girl trouble sweeps across the manor and Belphagor and Gurke find themselves on an awkward double-date.

programme.title: Wildest Islands
programme.desc: Islands can be home to the most extreme examples of life, the wildest of people and the most dramatic landscapes. Natural selection fuels evolution in the most dramatic way

programme.title: Wildest Europe
programme.desc: From mountain to sea, and from scorching desert to Arctic tundra, Europe’s great wilderness contains a stunning and surprising explosion of life. Wildest Europe reveals the incredible beauty and diversity of wildlife in Europe’s major natural habitats.

programme.title: Portillo's Hidden History Of Britain: Royal London Hospital
programme.desc: The Royal London Hospital pioneered medical breakthroughs.

programme.title: Portillo's Hidden History Of Britain: Orford Ness: Defence of the Realm
programme.desc: Orford Ness is a lens into an era of secrets and superpowers.

programme.title: Portillo's Hidden History Of Britain: Shepton Mallet
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores this 400-year-old prison.

programme.title: The Flying Scotsman: The Unstoppable Flying Scotsman
programme.desc: The Flying Scotsman has broken records and starred in movies.

programme.title: The Flying Scotsman
programme.desc: The mighty Scotsman becomes stranded stateside.

programme.title: The Railways That Built Britain: Boom, Bust and Blood
programme.desc: Chris Tarrant explores how railways transformed the nation.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: In this episode Tony travels to the lesser-known, North Eastern Coast of Jamaica. White sandy beaches and a cool breeze with a tropical umbrella drink in hand is usually the perception of island life.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: Libyan hip-hop, Italian restaurants, tribal allegiances and post-war uncertainty in Libya. Bourdain looks at the country through personal stories, food, and the music of anti-Qaddafi rapper expats.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: Once Johannesburg was the most dangerous city in the world, but the end of the apartheid has lead to vast changes in the city. Tony visits the Johannesburg suburb of Hillbrow, and discovers the culture and, of course food.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: Bourdain looks at the Congo as one of the more industrialized powers in Africa now largely crumbled to nothingness and chaos with Tim Butcher author of Blood River.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: With the slight relaxation of control by the government of Myanmar, Tony is finally able to explore one of the most fabled and beautiful areas of Asia.

programme.title: Anthony Bourdain Parts Unknown
programme.desc: Israel, the West Bank and Gaza are three locations Tony has never travelled to before.The intense and fraught relationship between these places is hard to understand but Tony will attempt to delve into the lives of those who live here, through their food.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Dog Is Smokin'
programme.desc: Dog is trying to cut down on smoking, but it's not easy with his high-stress job. Today, he must track down Walter, a fugitive who believes that the Canadian military has planted a chip in his tooth that allows him to communicate with his van.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: You Snooze, You Lose
programme.desc: Beth designs a luxurious new office for the team and shows it off to Dog, but the bounty hunter is more interested in starting his hunt for Federico, who's been revoked for missing court dates and generally running amuck.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Judgment Day
programme.desc: Dog goes on his a case, which involves Bobby, who's been on parole for three months pending sentencing on an assault charge. Bobby's girlfriend wants him back in jail, and Dog agrees that the Bobby needs to be behind bars.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Moms & Dads
programme.desc: Dog pursues two people whose lives have been ruined by drugs. First, he tracks Mae, who traded a good home and loving family for an ice pipe. The next case involves Jude. His addiction has caused his pregnant wife to fear for her safety.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Do Unto Others
programme.desc: Dog is a rough, tough bounty hunter, but he's a softie at heart, as the two cases in this episode show.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: A Helping Hand
programme.desc: Dog answers two very different calls for help from women. In the first case, he helps Leland protect Maria, who's being threatened by her violent husband. Then Dog visit a young woman who's dying and has asked to see Dog as one of her final wishes.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Take Your Daughter to Work Day
programme.desc: Baby Lyssa, who's just turned 18 and is studying to become a bondsman, gets to do some on-the-job training when she helps Dog hunt for Wayne, who's run out on a bond for second-degree assault.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: In Sickness and In Health
programme.desc: Dog is sick as a dog, but he still must lead the hunt for a fugitive named James. James' girlfriend tries to cover for him, but Dog surprises James in his bedroom and leads him away in cuffs.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: If the Shirt Fits...
programme.desc: Beth buys Dog a shirt with an embroidered chicken, but Dog explains he only wears shirts with pictures of either Jesus or a Harley-Davidson. When it's time to go to work, the team goes after Necia, a fugitive with felony charges pending.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: The Thief Who Stole Christmas
programme.desc: It's Christmas day, and Dog sets out to nab Raymond, who robs to support his ice habit, and give him a little Christmas gift - shiny new handcuffs.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Life of Si
programme.desc: After seeing footage Jep shot using a GoPro, Si decides he wants to use the camera to record a day in his life. But when Si gets on the guys nerves while they're hunting, they kick him and his GoPros out of the blind.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: From Duck 'til Dawn
programme.desc: When the duck guys have to pull an all-nighter to finish some work, Willie decides he wants to join them. But when a dying smoke detector distracts them from their work, Jase and Willie head off to disarm it.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Burger Commander
programme.desc: After visiting a popular burger restaurant, Willie and Jase get into a debate over which of them cooks the best burger, prompting them to challenge one another to a cooking contest.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: G.I. Si
programme.desc: When Willie and Jase argue over an old G.I. Joe toy, it leads them into a debate over who would make a better soldier. To settle the score, Willie and Jase head out to the woods with their friends and children for a paintball match.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Jase and the Argonauts
programme.desc: When Willie gets a soft serve machine for himself, he tells the guys they can't use it until they're done working. But when Jase thinks this is unfair, he takes matters into his own hands and finds a better object to use as motivation.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Fowl Playhouse
programme.desc: When Jase finds out Jep is planning to buy his kids a fancy playhouse from a catalogue, he and the guys decide to build one for Jep themselves. But the playhouse ends up looking more like a military-style duck blind.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: The Big LeCOWski
programme.desc: When Sadie spends the day at Duck Commander for a school project, the guys try to show her the ins-and-outs of how a successful company works. But when Willie questions Jase's ingenuity in creating new products they get into a debate.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: The Robertson family pulls together a family reunion to give Mia a big send-off before her surgery.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Governor's Travels
programme.desc: When the Governor of Louisiana announces that he will be coming to Duck Commander to present Willie with a commerce award, John Luke volunteers to give the introduction speech.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Quack and Gown
programme.desc: With Reed's graduation day approaching, Jase decides to take Reed frog hunting the night before, like he did on the day of his own graduation. Willie and Si join in, and Jase tries to keep them from imparting their "college advice" on Reed.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: With science closer than ever to proving that life once lived on Mars, is there a chance that something still exists there?

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Join us as we investigate the existence of UFOs and determine the possibility that they may be from Earth.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Explore the increase in UFO sightings that occurred during the spring of 1952.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Solar System UFOs: Discover the shocking truth about alien civilisations in our solar system. Check out derelict spacecrafts and explore the mysterious origins of UFOs.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Alien Bodies: Uncover the shocking truth about alien remains as experts investigate unsettling reports of extraterrestrial bodies recovered from crashed UFOs.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Signs of Abduction: From loss of time and bizarre hazy memories, to terrifying accounts of alien surgery, these are the hidden signs of alien abduction.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed: Episode 10

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Nazi UFOs: Could alien technology have helped the Nazis' rise to power? Experts question whether Hitler had extraterrestrial help in his quest for world domination.

programme.title: Military Machines: America in World War II
programme.desc: In the years leading up to WW2, Japan emerges as a military superpower in the Pacific, challenging America's isolationist stance.

programme.title: Military Machines: America in World War II
programme.desc: The US Army Air Corps was still flying bi-planes in the 1930s but would darken the skies over enemy cities with its might.

programme.title: Military Machines: America in World War II
programme.desc: From the smoldering wreckage of Pearl Harbor to final victory, this is the story of the US Navy and the two ocean war.

programme.title: Military Machines: America in World War II

programme.title: Secret Wars Uncovered: Syria

programme.title: Hitler's Engineers
programme.desc: As one of their most controversial scientists, Heisenberg was key in the Nazis' efforts to build an atomic bomb. How close was he to achieving his goal?

programme.title: The Abyss: Rise And Fall of The Nazis: Up in Flames 1936-1940

programme.title: History Of Weapons: The Ambush

programme.title: Road Wars

programme.title: Road Wars
programme.desc: In Woodland Hills, Calif., good Samaritans attempt to box in a driver involved in multiple wrecks near a freeway overpass; a woman in Louisiana is berated by another driver who believes she is not driving fast enough.

programme.title: Neighborhood Wars
programme.desc: A woman discovers a stranger looking in her window at night; a mother argues over the noise of her children's ATVs; a woman defends a street vendor selling cold drinks against a neighbor who wants the seller gone.

programme.title: Neighborhood Wars
programme.desc: After arguing with neighbors, a family awakes to discover their boat engulfed in flames; a couple is shocked when their security camera captures a naked neighbor; a man poking a street performer is surprised when the living cowboy statue retaliates.

programme.title: Neighborhood Wars
programme.desc: Two brothers confront a neighbor who they claim is spraying muddy water on their cars; a woman talks trash on her live feed as she has a neighbor's car towed out of her parking spot; clips of unidentified flying objects above neighborhoods.

programme.title: Neighborhood Wars
programme.desc: Good neighbors do good things.

programme.title: MetaJets

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Valley of the Iguanasquat / Legend of Wiggly's Gold
programme.desc: When the campers discover a prehistoric valley near Stinky Pits they also find a cursed gem.

programme.title: Magi-Nation
programme.desc: Vash Naroom is in flames. Our heroes are losing their battle against Agram. In his moment of victory, Agram reveals to Tony that he couldn't have escaped the Core without the assistance of one of his closest friends. Right then, Tony wakes up.

programme.title: Magi-Nation
programme.desc: Tony, Edyn, and Strag open the Book of Elders to the clue page for finding the Deeps of Orothe Dreamstone...when the page suddenly vanishes. Edyn recognizes it as a spell, which they can only break finding the Orothian who cast it.

programme.title: Magi-Nation
programme.desc: Tony and Edyn follow Strag to his home realm of the Underneath. They came to get some of Strag's old relics that could be useful in their search for the Underneath Dreamstone.

programme.title: Massive Monster Mayhem
programme.desc: Master Mayhem has created a new chart-topping pop trio: The Doo Doo Dooms! These three musical monsters will melt your heart, then melt your planet! Meanwhile Eye Eye Eye will do anything for some peace and quiet… like conquer Earth!

programme.title: MetaJets
programme.desc: The A-R-C is in Paris for a three day racing event, during the opening of the newest attraction - the new LOUVRE TOWER. When Johnny finds himself on a losing streak, he opts to try a new jet upgrade to give him an edge.

programme.title: MetaJets
programme.desc: Crime boss, Carmone Fontaine, father of Johnny’s friend Doug, decides to go straight as a way to win back his son’s respect, and the Metajets are brought in to escort him to New York, where he will surrender himself to authorities

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: Neutralized
programme.desc: Varis’ relentless onslaught continues as he pummels Playmaker into digital dust!

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: The Reveal
programme.desc: Varis reveals his true identity – as well as the terrifying truth behind the Ignis!

programme.title: Pink Panther & Pals: A Pinker Tomorrow
programme.desc: Pink Panther wakes up to find himself in a colorless future.

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'Pinkazuma's Revenge': Don Rosaro Pantero encounters Conquistadors, Aztecs and an uncooperative Parrot in the New World. 'Pinky Down Under': Australian bush guide Pinky is hired by two city slickers who try to poach the rare pygmy plaid kangaroo.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold are sent to find Merlin's magic inkwell which will grant the finder a wish. They go on a treasure hunt by following a series of clues.

programme.title: Count Duckula
programme.desc: Count Duckula is approached to perform as a prince in a Hollywood film.

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'Icy Pink': The Panther travels to Alaska to help an entrepreneurial Eskimo open up an ice cream factory. 'The End of Superpink': Super Pink has to prove to a little boy and his cat that it's brains, not brawn that determine a real superhero.

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: By using an electromagnetic pulse, VENOM melts everything electrical, which stops the Indy 500 and they get away with the ticket money.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Colonel K teams Danger Mouse up with a robot Penfold, and Penfold with a robot Danger Mouse. The robots prove to be a lot of trouble for the real ones, taking instructions too literally or getting stuck running in circles.

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'The Ghost and Mr. Panther': Pinky unknowingly takes a job as a house sitter…at a haunted house! 'Cleopanthra': Pinky wants to make it as an architect in ancient Egypt but must contend with the Royal Architect who is out to destroy his work.

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: At a special show for the Emir of Amrah, ten of the famous Lippizaner horses stampede and disappear.

programme.title: Bill & Ted's Excellent Adventures
programme.desc: Because of a Latin class assignment, Bill and Ted decide to travel to ancient Rome to acquire a rare coin that was minted during the reign of Julius Caesar. While there, they end up saving Caesar's life.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 8

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 20
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit an untouched property in Kent, a flat in East London with a tidy profit to be made and it never drains when it can pour at a house with some drainage problems in Lincoln.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 28
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a 3 bedroom property in Mid-Glamorgan, a house in London and an old telephone exchange in Devon.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 14

programme.title: Arthur: Desert Island Dish/The Secret About Secrets
programme.desc: When Mr. Ratburn challenges the class to find the perfect food for surviving on a desert island, everyone picks potato chips or ice cream! D.W. is told a secret and quickly learns that keeping one is a lot harder than she thought.

programme.title: Arthur: Buster's Green Thumb/My Fair Tommy
programme.desc: Buster finds out that his collection of old food makes excellent compost--which makes for even better tomatoes! Then, neither of the Tibble twins has ever won the coveted "Good Behavior of the Week" cupcake award.

programme.title: Arthur: When Duty Calls – Parts 1 & 2
programme.desc: Ladonna finds out the Army is transferring her dad to Oregon, and the whole Compson family is moving again. Fern and the others can tell Ladonna is upset, but don’t know how to help. What can they do to make the transition easier?

programme.title: Arthur: Three's a Crowd/A is for Angry
programme.desc: Prunella's loves doing yoga with her mom, but when her best friend joins them, will she be able to share her time? When Arthur decides to take on The Brain at a checkers competition, everyone rallies behind the underdog.

programme.title: Arthur: He Said, He Said/Bunny Trouble
programme.desc: A special episode of Bionic Bunny is interrupted, and Arthur and friends try to remember what comes next. D.W. is excited to be taking care of the class bunny, Larry. But Larry is feeling adventurous and and wanders off.

programme.title: Arthur: Baseball Blues/Brain's Biggest Blunder
programme.desc: It's Grebelings season and George can't wait to play! After all, no one knows more about baseball than George. Then, wBrain gets teamed with Buster and Binky in the annual Arithmattack competition.

programme.title: Arthur: D.W. Aims High/Flaw and Order
programme.desc: D.W. wants to be the first person to set foot on Mars! But what if scary purple aliens eat her? Who's responsible for cracking Dad's cake platter? DUN-DUN! All the evidence points to Arthur!

programme.title: Arthur: All Thumbs/Kidonia
programme.desc: When Arthur catches Buster sucking his thumb, Buster worries that Arthur will tell the entire school. Tired of doing chores, Arthur and friends decide to invent their own country. Will this kid-ruled utopia flourish or descend into anarchy?

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur's Dummy Disaster/Francine and The Feline
programme.desc: When painfully shy George brings a ventriloquist dummy to school, a popular kid is born! Then, Arthur thinks the best pet to have is a dog, and Francine probably would have agreed--that is, until little Nemo, a cute kitten, came along.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Marcos accidentally breaks an Art Show project that Melanie has made. A Park Kid named Myra has decided she doesn't like her name.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Let's Go For A Ride!
programme.desc: Barney and the children have fun playing with and learning about busses and cars.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: The Good Egg
programme.desc: When the kids find a mysterious, colorful egg in the park, Barney takes them on a trip to Kenya to try to find out what kind of animal could have laid the egg.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Big Brother Rusty
programme.desc: Barney's excited to find out that Rusty's family is adopting a new baby girl from China; however, Rusty feels differently. He's not sure if he's going to be a good brother, and he doesn't know much about China.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: BJ learns that having a pet is a bigger responsibility than he expected when he takes care of a friend’s dog. When Baby Bop’s favorite teddy bear is torn, she wants to take him to the vet for help.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Butterflies - Nature Of Things
programme.desc: Baby Bop is sad because she lost her pet caterpillar, Monty. Barney and the others try to help her find it. Baby Bop is surprised to discover Monty inside a chrysalis. Everyone is excited to find Monty—now a beautiful butterfly!

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Once Upon a Fairy Tale
programme.desc: Mario is trying to write his very own fairy tale. The children help by acting out some of their favorite fairy tales to give Mario some ideas.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Playing Games - No, No, No!
programme.desc: Baby Bop has trouble following the rules and taking turns while playing games. Barney and Riff help her learn how to follow the rules. In the end, Baby Bop and BJ have fun playing together, and Baby Bop learns that rules make games fun for everyone!

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Leonardo Versus Tempestra
programme.desc: Tempestra's Revenge, the toughest game in video arcade, has got Leonardo hooked. Just as he's finally about to beat the machine, lightening strikes, and an electrical overload brings the wicked Tempestra to life.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Splinter Vanishes!
programme.desc: First Master Splinter vanishes, leaving behind instructions that the Ninja Turtles must now disband and go their separate ways.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Raphael Drives Em' Wild
programme.desc: Raphael's brain inadvertently gets switched with that of Oscar the cab driver.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Beyond the Donatello Nebula
programme.desc: Donatello's efforts to contact life in the Turtle Nebula succeed only too well: he accidently transports a space-traveling lizard named Algy down to Earth.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Big Bug Blunder
programme.desc: After Bebop and Rocksteady ruin an experimental batch of mutagen, Krang discovers that this new goo grows giant-sized insects. Shredder immediately sends some of the humongous bugs to Earth.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Foot Soldiers are Revolting
programme.desc: Desperate for decent henchmen, Shredder decides to make some minions he can rely on, and uses Krang's brain wave enhancer on a robotic foot soldier.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Unidentified Flying Leonardo
programme.desc: Investigating reports of UFOs in the country town of High Falls, April uncovers a real scheme.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Turtles and the Hare
programme.desc: In Krang's latest scheme, Rocksteady and Bebop, dressed as Easter bunnies, are blasting people with a Docilizer Ray and turning them into timid, rabbit-like people.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Once Upon a Time Machine
programme.desc: Hokum Hare discovers that Shredder has a time train to travel to the future, where he can become crimelord of a crime-free era.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Michelangelo Meets Mondo Gecko
programme.desc: Michelangelo is turtle-napped by mutant lizard Mondo Gecko, who was mutated at the same time and place as the Turtles.

programme.title: That's 90s00s

programme.title: Total Drama Action

programme.title: Total Drama Action

programme.title: Total Drama Action

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: When a meteorite falls not far from the log cabin, strange berries start to grow. One of their properties is that they make anyone who eats them grow to disproportionate sizes.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Under the wood floor of the log cabin, Grizzy and the Lemmings discover the entrance to an underground gallery with an old treasure card that indicates the location of a treasure.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: When he brandishes an Indian sceptre that the Lemmings brought home with other antique objects, Grizzy sees the Lemmings bow low before him and behave like servants.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy tries to impress the she-bear that he's in love with by making a sculpture of her from a piece of wood. But as soon as the artist's back is turned, the Lemmings gnaw at the work of art in order to transform it into a toboggan slide.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings throw a surprise birthday party for one of the clan. When Happy Birthday rings out, the surprise is as great for Grizzy, who is trying to take a nap after a hearty meal, as it is for the birthday Lemming hero in question.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: A gust of wind sweeps away the flower that Grizzly's adored she-bear wears in her hair. Grizzly sees this as a golden opportunity to dazzle her by bringing it back.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy finds two identical vases that are actually communicating vessels in a Native American Indian chest. If he sticks his hand in one, it comes out the other, regardless of the distance between them.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy finds a vintage stopwatch in the garage which lets one wind back the minute that has just passed by, and it can even stop time and movement in one's surroundings.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy is disturbed in the middle of his TV snack by a flow of incongruous objects that have been transformed into hot-air balloons.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy discovers with great delight that the ranger has purchased a brand new multi-function Stressless recliner. But the Lemmings transform it into a fearsome multi-game chair.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings play kite surf with a homemade kite. Grizzly notices that the she-bear he's in love with watches the kite perform arabesques in the sky with admiration.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy snatches the last jar of chocolate spread out from under the noses of the Lemmings. In exchange, he offers them the toy stuck inside the lid, a mini-flute.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings play a new game, floating like flags in the breeze from the fan set on maximum. But Grizzy quickly rids the living room of this interference.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have a grand old time tearing down their Kapla block buildings, which they rebuild just as fast. Grizzly remarks that the she-bear he's in love with is impressed by the Lemmings' construction and makes believe that it's his work of art.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings draw inspiration from lessons they find in a martial arts book and start breaking every wooden object they can get their hands on into two. When Grizzy tries to stop them, they go on to the next lesson and get him in a judo hold.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy dreams that he's a valiant knight who has delivered his beloved. As he is about to take her in his arms, he is woken by the Lemmings who have chosen this day to blast music.

programme.title: Pokemon: Advanced Battle

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies sit down to eat some Tubby Custard, but they stop to watch some children buy some different sorts of vegetables. The Teletubbies ask the question – what colour is Tubby Custard?

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children perform a Maori Song. In Teletubbyland, Po sings a lovely song. Gradually the other Teletubbies join in, until they sing a lovely song together. The Teletubbies love to sing.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children wearing their boots and playing in the garden. A pair of boots appears in Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children mixing water, paint and washing up liquid to make bubble pictures. Po shows us how to make Tubby Custard before running off to watch three ships sail through Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies pop up to say "Eh-oh", and then run off to watch two children go for a ride in a hovercraft. The Teletubbies are very careful not to drop their Tubby blankets on the floor, but if they do, the Noo Noo tidies up.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children learn about the number two. Dipsy and Laa-Laa are eating Tubby Toast.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Andy Brown and some children feed some birds and watch them playing on the bird table. In Teletubbyland, it's timer for Tubby Custard, but Dipsy spills his all over a Tubby Seat. No-one wants to sit in the Tubby Custard, so Noo-Noo tidies it up.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some children who love to skip. In Teletubbyland, the Teletubbies practice skipping. To begin with they’re not very good, but after lots of practice, they skip all over Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch some Spanish children picking chillies. In Teletubbyland the Tubby Toast machine wasn’t working. Tubby Toast flies everywhere.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies go up the slide to see two children help mend a dry stone wall. A wall appears in Teletubbyland. Laa-Laa, Po and Tinky Winky play a game with the wall. They hear Dipsy coming and hide behind the wall.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MYSTERIOUS TEXTS
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at mysteries related to ancient texts including the Egyptian Book of the Dead, the Torah, the Secrets of Fulcanelli, the Tao Te Ching, the Vedas.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MYSTERIES OF ANCIENT CULTURES
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at mysteries associated with ancient cultures including Lemuria, the Indus Valley civilization of Harappa, the Forgotten Kingdoms of Tibet, the Olmec civilization of Central America.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: RELICS OF THE SAINT
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at the relics of the saints including the Shrine of the Three Kings, the Tilman of Our Lady of Guadalupe, the Holy Foreskin, the Ark of the Covenant, the Remain of Mary Magdalene, Buddha's Tooth, and the Holy Grail.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SECRET CULTS AND ORDERS
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at secret cults and mysterious orders including Skull and Bones at Yale University, the Freemasons, Thule and Vril Societies, the Bullingdon Club, the Hermetic Order of the Golden Dawn

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SCIENCE AND MYSTERIES FOR 21. CENTURY
programme.desc: Top 10 Secrets and Mysteries looks at some of the mysteries of modern science including memory of water, reincarnation, the beginning of life, hypnosis, the secrets of Morphic fields, time travel, why we must sleep.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: PEOPLE WITHOUT IDENTITY
programme.desc: 10 Mysterious Figures: Individuals who emerged from obscurity, made their mark on history, then vanished without a trace. Their identities remain unconfirmed, shrouding them in mystery.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: NATURE’S MAGIC
programme.desc: Natural sites worldwide hold spiritual significance for civilizations, from ancient rituals to modern reverence, each crackling with unique energy and a hint of magic.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MYSTERIOUS WORKS OF ART
programme.desc: Great art and its creators unveil mysteries more intricate than TV dramas. Amidst theft and intrigue, we persist in solving them. Yet, the truth often lies buried with those who created it, adding layers of intrigue to the art world.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SECRETS AND MAGIC OF ALTERNATIVE MEDICINE
programme.desc: In this episode, we dive into untraditional healing and its mysteries, challenging conventional Western medicine. Explore alternative medical miracles that will captivate you.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Natural History Museum, London
programme.desc: From Darwin’s original specimens to a lost workshop that inspired James Bond, discover the secrets of Britain’s Natural History Museum.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Imperial War Museum, London

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Topkapi Palace Museum, Istanbul

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the American Museum of Natural History, New York
programme.desc: From a dinosaur embryo that solves a murder mystery to a spacecraft catching a comet’s tail, discover the secrets inside the American Museum of Natural History.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Louvre
programme.desc: From murdered kings to stolen masterpieces, discover the secrets inside the Louvre.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Palacio Real, Madrid
programme.desc: From a clock that runs on witchcraft to a 21st Century king who outfoxes a fascist, discover the secrets inside the Palacio Real.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: De-Bug Life
programme.desc: The guys realize it is time for the annual cleaning of the duck blinds when they come across a gator using one as a home. Meanwhile, while the kids are running the church car wash, Si and Willie disagree on the best way to draw in customers.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Men vs. Wild

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Return of the Beavers
programme.desc: When Phil discovers more beaver activity on the Robertson land, he recruits Jase, Jep, and Si to get rid of them. Meanwhile, Willie and Korie bring Bella to her first day of karate practice.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Stumped on a way to celebrate Willie's birthday, Korie enlists the guys' help and Jase takes the reigns in planning a trip to the lake where Phil and Kay used to live. Meanwhile, the women realize a raccoon is inside the lake house.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Glory Is the Reward of Mallard
programme.desc: When Willie takes a business trip to Scotland to expand his duck call business, he brings the entire family along so they can reconnect with their Scottish roots. Willie organizes a bus tour to trace his family heritage.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed: Episode 11

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Between 1981 and 2011, cameras on NASA space shuttles have picked up many images of UFOs that experts still struggle to explain.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed: Episode 9
programme.desc: We have always been told that mankind enjoys free will. Is this really true or have aliens created the means for controlling humanity?

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: Discover theories relating to why aliens are interested in visiting Earth, including the allures of the human mind and human civilisation.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: As the world continues to evolve into the digital age, people are capturing images in the sky that they are unable to explain.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: While many UFO sightings occur in rural locations with only a single eyewitness, there have been sightings over cities witnessed by hundreds.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed: Episode 8
programme.desc: Take a fresh look at some classic alien encounters, UFOs and abductions, and discover several new stories.

programme.title: Alien Files Unsealed
programme.desc: An in-depth look at the US government's classified and declassified investigations into UFO sightings.

programme.title: Military Machines: America in World War II

programme.title: Military Machines: America in World War II
programme.desc: This is the story of the creation of the Allied military from the small 100,000-man force of the post World War I era to the mighty military industrial complex that fought fascist forces around the world. 10 Hour Series

programme.title: Military Machines: America in World War II
programme.desc: The US Coast Guard was the first branch of the US military to see action in WWII.

programme.title: Military Machines: America in World War II
programme.desc: The US Coast Guard saw extensive action throughout the Second World War, battling U-boats within sight of mainland America.

programme.title: Battle of Britain

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: In 1943, the inmates of the Death Camp at Sobibor staged an organised rebellion against their Nazi oppressors.

programme.title: U-Boat Wargamers: The Game

programme.title: Hell On Earth WWII

programme.title: Auschwitz Countdown To Liberation
programme.desc: An exceptional documentary that chronicles the liberation of Auschwitz, commencing on the day of liberation and backtracking in time to narrate the tragedy of the Holocaust.

programme.title: Wicked Tuna: The Mighty Bite
programme.desc: With only two weeks left in the fishing season and the price of blue fin tuna on the rise, the Gloucester captains race to bring in record hauls.

programme.title: Wicked Tuna: First Blood

programme.title: Wicked Tuna: Anchor Anger

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur's Dummy Disaster/Francine and The Feline
programme.desc: When painfully shy George brings a ventriloquist dummy to school, a popular kid is born! Then, Arthur thinks the best pet to have is a dog, and Francine probably would have agreed--that is, until little Nemo, a cute kitten, came along.

programme.title: Arthur: Francine's Big Top Trouble/George Blows His Top
programme.desc: Francine is sure she will be a star at circus camp. How hard could it be? Then, George is always more than happy to lend his pals whatever they need. But Buster's borrowing gets out of control.

programme.title: Arthur: Mom and Dad Have A Great Big Fight/D.W.'s Perfect Wish
programme.desc: When Mom and Dad fight over spilled milk, Arthur and D.W. fear the worst. Is it Arthur's fault or D.W.'s? Then, turning five is a major, big deal event! But D.W. worries that she fribbled away her preschool years on meaningless pursuits.

programme.title: Arthur: Fern and the Case of the Stolen Story/Sue Ellen Vegges Out
programme.desc: Fern has writer's block! Fortunately, Ladonna's full of stories, and Fern decides to "borrow" the tales and simply change the names. Then, when Sue Ellen decides to stop eating meat, Muffy and Francine compete to be the best vegetarian.

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur's Tooth/D.W. Gets Lost
programme.desc: It's bad enough to wear glasses all the time. But does Arthur have to be the last person in his class to lose his baby teeth? Then, if you think being lost in a humongous department store would scare a four-year-old, think again.

programme.title: Arthur: D.W. Thinks Big/Arthur Cleans Up
programme.desc: Once again, Arthur gets to do everything. Mostly he gets to carry the ring in Aunt Lucy's wedding. Elwood City's park is in sad shape, and after grumbling about it, the kids find themselves volunteered to clean it up.

programme.title: Arthur: Hide And Snake/Muffy's New Best Friend
programme.desc: The kids capture a snake and learn that it's possibly poisonous--after it gets lost in Arthur's house! Why is Muffy interviewing for a new best friend? She and Francine are usually inseparable, but lately it seems they have less in common.

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur Read: Super Saver!/Tibbles to the Rescue!
programme.desc: Business is slow for Mr. and Mrs. Read, and Arthur is concerned. Can he find ways to keep his family in the green? When D.W. saves Tommy and Timmy from a nasty fall, they decide to repay the favor…whether D.W. likes it or not.

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur Makes Waves/It Came From Beyond
programme.desc: With the community pool closed for repairs and the temperature on the rise, Arthur and D.W. need a place to keep cool. Then, Grandma Thora takes in a stray: an adorable little dog named...Killer?

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Playing Games - No, No, No!
programme.desc: Baby Bop has trouble following the rules and taking turns while playing games. Barney and Riff help her learn how to follow the rules. In the end, Baby Bop and BJ have fun playing together, and Baby Bop learns that rules make games fun for everyone!

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Gift Of The Dinos - Winter
programme.desc: It's Christmas and BJ dreams of getting a new baseball glove to go with his baseball, while Riff hopes for new drumsticks to use his drum. Barney reminds them that Christmas is more than just getting gifts and helps them learn the meaning of Christmas.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: The Park Kids decide to have a Dog Show to show off their pets. A rainy day has everyone stuck in the Caboose. Barney suggests fun activities.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: The Big Garden - Get Happy
programme.desc: Baby Bop wants Barney, BJ and the kids to play with her instead of working in their garden. They promise to play with her as soon as they finish. Baby Bop can't wait, and tries many ways to get them to come play with her now, none of which work.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Sharing Is Caring!
programme.desc: The children bring toys to donate to Mr. Boyd's toy drive. Baby Bop and BJ arrive and after some convincing, Baby Bop learns about the merits of sharing.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Play Piano With Me!
programme.desc: The children learn all about the piano when they play games, listen to real pianos and even play a little themselves.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Home Safe Home
programme.desc: Safety first! After learning about fire safety when a firefighter visits the caboose, Barney and his friends are inspired to do a little "safety inspection" of their own.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: It's A Happy Day!
programme.desc: Beth has invented a brand-new holiday called "Happy Day," in which everyone gets to make a wish for something which will make them happy—and everyone else tries to make that wish come true! Barney and the kids discuss feelings and facial expressions.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Stop! Go!
programme.desc: After playing with "Stop" and "Go" signs with Angela, Baby Bop leaves to direct some traffic of her own! Meanwhile, in the caboose, Barney and the kids enjoy a costume parade, a game of "Musical Stop & Go," and some very vigorous body movement.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Michelangelo Meets Mondo Gecko
programme.desc: Michelangelo is turtle-napped by mutant lizard Mondo Gecko, who was mutated at the same time and place as the Turtles.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Enter: Mutagen Man
programme.desc: Krang plans to use a large rocket's vapour trail to rain mutagen over the city when nerdy delivery boy Seymour Guts falls into Shredder's mutagen vat.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Donatello's Badd Time
programme.desc: Hillybilly crooks Bedelia Badd and sons Bufrod and Bubbaheist the Turtles new improved van, using it to steal the diamond in Dr. Kepple's Powercizer, which drains the city's power and could destroy it.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Michelangelo Meets Bugman - Again!
programme.desc: The Swatter threatens to destroy the city with a stolen swarm of super termites unless the Turtles meet him atop The Entire State Building.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Muckman Messes Up!
programme.desc: Krang sends Shredder, Rocksteady and Bebop after new mutagen Compound X-7, which Krang thinks will un-mutate Turtles. At lab, garbagemen are doused with the stuff and change into mutants Muckman and Joe Eyeball, blaming Turtles.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Napoleon Bullfrog: Colossus of the Swamps
programme.desc: Krang uses Big Louie to steal anti-freeze to try to start the Technodrome, now stuck in the Arctic. Shredder zaps Turtle pal Napoleon Bullfrog, with mutagen, who mutates into a giant monster and escapes into the city.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Raphael vs. The Volcano
programme.desc: Raphael, convinced he is dying due to a faulty reading by Donatell's Health-O-Meter, becomes do-gooder THE Green Defender, popping up around the city foiling crime.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Donatello's Duplicate
programme.desc: Donatello creates an egotistical Clone of himself who stuffs Donatello in a trunk.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Ice Creature Cometh
programme.desc: Bebop dumps a vat of mutagen into the Arctic ice, creating a gigantic Ice Monster, who Krang unleashes on the city to help him steal thermal nuclear devices to free the Technodrome.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Leonardo Cuts Loose
programme.desc: Muscleman fiend Wally Airhead captures Turtles with a runaway exercise machine trap to add their powers to his race of super musclemen, but Turtles escape.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy dreams that he's a valiant knight who has delivered his beloved. As he is about to take her in his arms, he is woken by the Lemmings who have chosen this day to blast music.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy stands in front of the pantry, desperate. The stock of chocolate spread is completely depleted. He realises that the Lemmings are making some themselves, following a complicated recipe.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Inspired by a magazine with a jewellery ad showing a man offering a diamond to a woman, Grizzy imagines himself offering the ranger's engagement ring to his beloved she-bear.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: When he accidentally sticks a computer microprocessor to his temple, Grizzy is transformed into an exceptionally intelligent being capable of predicting events to come far in advance.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings prepare a dream afternoon snack with the last jar of chocolate spread in the cupboard. Tough luck, Grizzy is coming. Caught off guard, the Lemmings hide the jar in a teddy bear-pyjama case to slip away without arousing suspicion.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy gleefully discovers that he can print images taken with the ranger's camera as it's connected to a state-of-the-art printer. He decides to frame himself, but when the time comes to print his portrait, there's no more paper in the printer.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: A van pulls up to the ranger's cabin to deliver a full crate of jars of chocolate spread. While Grizzy and the Lemmings fight over a wrecking bar to open it, someone steals the crate.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: As usual, Grizzly picks the door of the Ranger's log cabin to come in and make himself comfortable, but this time it's impossible to enter. The Lemmings have requisitioned the cabin for a party and barricaded themselves inside.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings have got their hands on some white beans which have very strange properties, when mixed with soda anyone who eats them turns into a bouncing ball. Grizzy realises that if he wants to have a nap, he must get rid of the magic beans.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: Grizzy wants to get rid of the old piano in the garage at any cost, for the Lemmings love using it to make a dissonant racket. But when he notices a she-bear he's in love with likes the melody of Fur Elise, he decides to learn the piece.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings inherit a crate that fell off a Canadian army convoy transporting ultra-secret material. The case inside the crate emits a sort of laser beam that divides objects into multiple miniaturised versions of themselves.

programme.title: Shasha & Milo
